cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question about Wu Jinyou. The passage is from a news article about Chinese students visiting the Yellow River Station in Norway's Arctic. The question asks which statement is true about Wu Jinyou based on the text. First, I need to find all mentions of Wu Jinyou in the passage. Let me scan through the text again. The relevant part is toward the end. It says: "Wu Jinyou, head of the Polar Research Institute of China, and the group's leader, said, 'I hope the students have not only a field trip to the Yellow River station, but are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here. I also expect in the future, some of them will choose to devote themselves to polar research and maybe even work with us.'" So from this, we know Wu Jinyou is the head of the Polar Research Institute of China and the leader of the group. His comments express that he values the devotion and spirit of the researchers and hopes some students might pursue polar research. Now let's look at the options: (A) He is a graduate from Dalian Marinetime University. - The passage mentions Zheng Li as a student from Dalian Marinetime University, but there's no information about Wu Jinyou's education. So this is not supported. (B) He is the president of Dalian Marinetime University. - Again, the article mentions Dalian Marinetime University in relation to Zheng Li, but there's no indication that Wu Jinyou is the president there. His title is head of the Polar Research Institute of China. This is incorrect. (C) He thinks highly of the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers. - This is directly stated in his quote: "are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here."
